# Figure description: Геологическая_структура_Шабдарской_структуры.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Общие_данные_для_всех_листов/Геологическая_структура_Шабдарской_структуры.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **schematic geological map** (Схематическая геологическая карта) at a scale of 1:50,000, presented in colour with a detailed legend. It is a surface/near-surface bedrock geology map with structural contours, fault traces, stratigraphic boundaries, and borehole locations marked. Drawing number **Чертеж №08** is noted in the upper right corner.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers the **Шабдарская структура** (Shabdar Structure), a named geological/tectonic structure in northern Kazakhstan. The scale is **1:50,000**, making this a relatively detailed local map. The map was compiled by **Пулецков Г.В.** and **Смолянинова Е.Н.** (Pulyetskov G.V. and Smolyaninova E.N.), consistent with the authorship of Report 36572. Several borehole locations are plotted on the map (including what appears to be **скв-150** and others), and formation codes visible include Silurian, Devonian, Carboniferous, Neogene-Paleogene, and Quaternary units. The map also references the **Западно-Тенизская ОСВ** (Zapadno-Tenizskaya reference seismic survey line) in the legend.

---

## Key Content

### Structural Geology
The map depicts an **anticlinal or domal structure** — the Shabdar Structure — visible as a roughly oval/elongate closure trending NW–SE in the central portion of the map. The core of the structure is coloured **pink/salmon**, representing older (Devonian) rocks, flanked by progressively younger units (Carboniferous in grey-blue) toward the periphery, which is the classic pattern of an eroded anticline exposing older rocks in the core.

### Colour Scheme and Formations
- **Pink/salmon (central core):** Devonian System, Upper Division — Famennian suite, red-coloured and variegated sandstones, siltstones, and conglomerates (Даррнская свита — Darrninskaya Suite).
- **Dotted pink (adjacent to core):** Devonian System, Middle–Upper Division — Khovosnitsvetiye (variegated) conglomerates, sandstones, tuffs, and tuffites.
- **Green (small patch, NW of core):** Carboniferous System — Visean Stage (limestone marker).
- **Blue-grey (surrounding the Devonian core):** Carboniferous System in multiple subdivisions — including Lower Carboniferous (Tournaisian, Visean, Serpukhovian) and Middle Carboniferous (Bashkirian, Moscovian) horizons. These are described as sandstones, aleurolites, organogenic and biomorphic limestones, marls, and clays.
- **Yellow (SW and outer areas):** Quaternary deposits (Holocene–Pleistocene) — loams, sands, clays, gravels.
- **Light yellow (larger southern area):** Neogene-Paleogene — Maikop-type green and variegated ryabinovy (ash-coloured) clays.
- **Light blue (eastern and southeastern areas):** Carboniferous units extending beyond the structure, with various formation codes (C₁, C₂, etc.).

### Structural Symbols
- **Solid black lines:** Fault/rupture dislocations (Разрывные нарушения) — several faults are mapped cutting through and around the structure, some with tick marks indicating downthrown side.
- **Dashed lines:** Stratigraphic boundaries (Стратиграфические границы).
- **Dotted contour patterns:** Sandstone beds — grey and red-coloured (Пласты песчаников, серостветных и красноцветных).
- **Wavy line patterns:** Conglomerate beds (Пласты конгломератов).
- **Dashed-dot patterns:** Limestone beds (Пласты известняков) and porous/cavernous limestones (Пласты дырчатых известняков — literally "holey limestones," i.e., vuggy/cavernous).
- **Special marker horizon:** A **marking limestone bed with skeletal fauna** (Маркирующий пласт окремнелых известняков с скелетной фауной) is identified — this is a key stratigraphic marker.
- **Silicified limestones with flint nodules** (Пласты известняков с линзами и желваками кремня) are also distinguished.
- **Igneous/intrusive rocks** are implied by some symbols but not dominant.
- **Borehole symbols:** Circles with lines = vertical wells; angled symbols = inclined/directional wells (Рекомендуемые скважины вертикальные и наклонные — recommended vertical and inclined boreholes). Some existing boreholes from the Zapadno-Tenizskaya line are shown (Скважины Западно-Тенизской ОСВ — numbered, with the well number to the right).

### Faulting
Multiple **NW–SE and NE–SW trending faults** dissect the structure, with some appearing to bound the anticlinal core. The intersection of two fault trends may create a **rhombic or trapezoidal structural block** in the core.
